key_insight: The paper shows that preference-shaped expected improvement for multiobjective Bayesian optimization depends critically on geometry: hypervolume-based criteria and R2-based criteria preserve different exactness and monotonicity properties under different transformations.
engineering_relevance: This clarifies when surrogate-based multiobjective optimizers can be computed exactly and when a preference transformation changes the meaning of the acquisition function, affecting algorithm design and reliability.
method: Revisits EHVI using the Deng representation, including product-density weighted and cone-transformed variants.
Proves that exact integral R2 improvement is not generally an objective-space hypervolume, but a scalarization-space volume in Tchebycheff space.
Derives finite-sum discrete ER2I algorithms, quadrature methods for exact integral R2, and a Gaussian surrogate formulation for achievement-space ER2I.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports exact geometric representations and theoretical results on monotonicity and Pareto compatibility, rather than an empirical benchmark study.
watch_item: Truncated EHVI can break variance monotonicity, so preference shaping must be checked carefully before using it as an acquisition function.
paper_title: Preference-Shaped Expected Hypervolume and R2 Improvement: Exact Computation and Monotonicity
